implementation.name: read_tasks
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: API endpoint to retrieve all tasks. Supports listing tasks for management.
implementation.pseudo_code:
When a GET request is made to /tasks:
    Call database.get_tasks()
    Return the list of tasks

implementation.name: get_tasks
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Reads all tasks from the persistent storage (tasks.json). Used by endpoints to fetch tasks.
implementation.pseudo_code:
If tasks.json does not exist:
    Return empty list
Open tasks.json
Try to load JSON data
    For each item, create a Task object
    Return list of Task objects
If JSON decode fails:
    Return empty list

implementation.name: create_task
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: API endpoint to add a new task. Receives all required fields and delegates creation to the database layer.
implementation.pseudo_code:
When a POST request is made to /tasks with TaskCreate data:
    Call database.add_task(task)
    Return the created Task

implementation.name: add_task
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Creates a new Task with all required fields and saves it. Ensures all fields from TaskCreate are present.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Get all tasks
Create a new Task with a unique id and fields from TaskCreate
Append new Task to tasks list
Save tasks list to tasks.json
Return the new Task

implementation.name: update_task
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: API endpoint to edit an existing task. Receives updated fields and delegates update to the database layer.
implementation.pseudo_code:
When a PUT request is made to /tasks/{task_id} with TaskCreate data:
    Call database.update_task(task_id, task)
    If updated_task is None:
        Raise 404 error
    Return updated_task

implementation.name: update_task
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Updates an existing task with new data. Ensures all fields are updated as per TaskCreate.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Get all tasks
For each task in tasks:
    If task.id matches task_id:
        Create updated Task with new data
        Replace old task with updated Task
        Save tasks list
        Return updated Task
If not found, return None

implementation.name: delete_task
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: API endpoint to delete a task by id.
implementation.pseudo_code:
When a DELETE request is made to /tasks/{task_id}:
    Call database.delete_task(task_id)
    If not success:
        Raise 404 error
    Return success message

implementation.name: delete_task
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Removes a task from the persistent storage by id.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Get all tasks
Filter out the task with matching id
If a task was removed:
    Save updated tasks list
    Return True
Else:
    Return False

missing_implementations: Mark tasks as Completed or Pending

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Provides the user interface for creating and editing tasks. Handles input for title, description, priority, due date, and category (tag). Validates required fields and triggers submission logic.
implementation.name: TaskForm
implementation.pseudo_code:
Render form with fields: title (required), description, priority, category, due date
On submit:
    IF title is empty
        Prevent submission
    ELSE
        Call onSubmit with task data
        (title, description, priority, category, due date, completed)
On cancel:
    Call onCancel

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Handles sending task creation requests from the frontend to the backend API. Ensures task data is sent in the correct format and handles response/error.
implementation.name: api.createTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
Function createTask(task):
    Send POST request to /tasks endpoint with task data
    IF response is not OK
        Throw error
    ELSE
        Return created task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Defines the backend API endpoint for task creation. Receives task data, validates it, and delegates to database logic for persistence.
implementation.name: create_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
POST /tasks endpoint:
    Receive TaskCreate object
    Call database.add_task(task)
    Return created Task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Implements the logic for persisting a new task. Generates unique ID, saves task to JSON file, and returns the new task object.
implementation.name: add_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
Function add_task(task_create):
    Get current tasks
    Generate new task with unique ID
    Append new task to tasks list
    Save tasks to file
    Return new task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Provides UI for displaying and editing tasks. Supports editing by invoking onEdit, which can trigger TaskForm for editing existing tasks.
implementation.name: TaskItem
implementation.pseudo_code:
Render task details (title, description, priority, category, due date)
Render buttons: Done/Undo, Edit, Delete
On Edit:
    Call onEdit(task) to open TaskForm for editing

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Handles sending task update requests from the frontend to the backend API. Used for editing tasks after creation.
implementation.name: api.updateTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
Function updateTask(id, task):
    Send PUT request to /tasks/{id} endpoint with updated task data
    IF response is not OK
        Throw error
    ELSE
        Return updated task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Defines the backend API endpoint for updating tasks. Receives updated task data, validates, and delegates to database logic.
implementation.name: update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
PUT /tasks/{task_id} endpoint:
    Receive TaskCreate object
    Call database.update_task(task_id, task)
    IF task not found
        Return 404 error
    ELSE
        Return updated task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Implements the logic for updating an existing task. Finds task by ID, updates fields, saves to file, and returns updated task.
implementation.name: update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
Function update_task(task_id, task_update):
    Get current tasks
    FOR each task in tasks
        IF task.id == task_id
            Update task fields
            Save tasks to file
            Return updated task
    Return None if not found

missing_implementations: Input validation for required fields beyond frontend (e.g., backend validation for title, priority, due date)
Support for custom tags beyond fixed categories (Work, Personal, Study) in UI and backend

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Provides the UI trigger for editing an existing task. Calls onEdit with the task object, initiating the edit flow.
implementation.name: TaskItem (Edit Button)
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF user clicks 'Edit' button on a task
    CALL onEdit(task)
    OPEN TaskForm with initialTask set to task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Displays a form pre-filled with task attributes for editing. Handles changes to title, description, priority, due date, and tag/category. On submit, calls onSubmit with updated values.
implementation.name: TaskForm
implementation.pseudo_code:
DISPLAY form with fields: title, description, priority, category, due date
IF initialTask exists
    PREFILL fields with initialTask values
ON form submit
    CALL onSubmit(updatedTask)

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Sends a PUT request to the backend to update the task with all edited attributes. Connects UI form submission to backend endpoint.
implementation.name: api.updateTask
implementation.pseudo_code:
CALL fetch('/tasks/{id}', method='PUT', body=updatedTask)
IF response is OK
    RETURN updated task
ELSE
    THROW error

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Receives PUT requests to /tasks/{task_id}. Calls backend logic to update the task. Returns updated task or 404 if not found.
implementation.name: update_task (FastAPI endpoint)
implementation.pseudo_code:
ON PUT /tasks/{task_id}
    CALL database.update_task(task_id, task)
    IF updated_task is None
        RETURN 404 error
    ELSE
        RETURN updated_task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Updates the task in the database with new values for title, description, priority, category, due date, and completed status. Saves changes to storage.
implementation.name: update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
LOAD tasks from storage
FOR each task in tasks
    IF task.id == task_id
        REPLACE task attributes with values from task_update
        SAVE tasks to storage
        RETURN updated task
RETURN None if not found
